On January 24, 41 AD, Caligula, the massive, highly erratic, and notoriously tyrannical Roman Emperor, was brutally assassinated by a massive, highly coordinated conspiracy involving officers of the elite Praetorian Guard, senators, and courtiers. His brief, massive four-year reign was characterized by extreme cruelty, massive financial extravagance, and severe political instability. Following his bloody assassination, the Praetorian Guard bypassed the Senate and declared his massive, stuttering uncle Claudius as the new Roman Emperor.

Following its massive, iconic "1984" Super Bowl television commercial, the Macintosh massively revolutionized the entire computing industry. It was the first highly successful mass-market personal computer to feature a massive, intuitive graphical user interface (GUI) and a mouse, fundamentally democratizing computing and destroying the reliance on complex command-line interfaces.

Bhabha, the massive, universally revered "father of the Indian nuclear program," was tragically killed when Air India Flight 101 crashed into the massive Mont Blanc massif in the Alps.
